steps.title: Calculate the new angle after rotation
steps.content: Point A is at (0, 1), which is at angle $\frac{\pi}{2}$ on the unit circle. Rotating counterclockwise by $\frac{7\pi}{6}$ adds this angle to the current angle: $$\theta = \frac{\pi}{2} + \frac{7\pi}{6} = \frac{3\pi}{6} + \frac{7\pi}{6} = \frac{10\pi}{6} = \frac{5\pi}{3}$$

steps.title: Use the unit circle formula to find coordinates
steps.content: For a point on the unit circle at angle $\theta$, the coordinates are $(\cos \theta, \sin \theta)$. At $\theta = \frac{5\pi}{3}$ (in the fourth quadrant): $$\cos\left(\frac{5\pi}{3}\right) = \frac{1}{2}, \quad \sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{3}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$ Therefore, $P = \left(\frac{1}{2}, -\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
